terms.definition: The systemic property describing how tightly the social, informational, and moral practices of a population form a mutually reinforcing whole, yielding low internal friction and high predictability
A derived metric equal to the distance over which news can propagate divided by the time required for that propagation (IP = Δx / Δt)
A non-linear shift in collective behavior that occurs when a driving parameter crosses a critical threshold, analogous to state changes in statistical physics
A culturally transmitted temporal orientation favoring deferred gratification and long planning horizons; treated as an emergent moral rate-limiter in the framework
